lifecycle.name: NIGHTLY_AUTONOMOUS_RUN
lifecycle.summary: On-demand pre-flight gate for GitHub issues labeled autonomously-nightly. Scans open issues, correlates with beads tickets (creating one if needed), assesses completeness, then either activates autonomous mode on the standard workflow or posts clarifying questions and exits.

phase.name: DETECT

actions.action: Query GitHub for open issues labeled "autonomously-nightly": gh issue list --label "autonomously-nightly" --state open --json number,title,body,labels,createdAt --limit 20 If the command fails, report the error and exit. If no issues are returned, report that no issues are labeled for nightly processing and exit.
Select the oldest issue by createdAt (one-at-a-time processing). Store the issue number, title, and body for subsequent phases.
Check .beads/github-map.json for a beads ticket mapped to this GitHub issue number. (Reverse lookup: scan values for the issue number, return the matching key/ticket ID.)

If a mapped beads ticket exists: Read the ticket with "bd show {ticket-id}" to confirm it still exists and is valid. If the ticket no longer exists (bd show fails), treat as unmapped and fall through to MIGRATE_ISSUE.

If the mapped beads ticket exists but is CLOSED (all work done): Read the GitHub issue comments to check for follow-up work: gh issue view {number} --json comments --jq '.comments[].body' Scan the comments for: - New feature requests or enhancements beyond the original scope - Bug reports or issues with the completed work - Explicit requests for additional changes - Review feedback that requires code changes Ignore: - Bot comments, CI status updates, migration notices - Comments that are purely informational or acknowledgments - Comments that predate the ticket's close date

If follow-up work IS found in the comments: Summarize the follow-up work from the comments into a new ticket description. Proceed to MIGRATE_ISSUE to create a new beads ticket for the follow-up work. The new ticket title should reference the original (e.g., "Follow-up: {original title}"). The new ticket description should include the relevant comment content and a link back to the original GitHub issue for context.

If NO follow-up work is found in the comments (ticket is closed, work is done): Remove the "autonomously-nightly" label so the issue is not picked up again: gh issue edit {number} --remove-label "autonomously-nightly" Do NOT close the GitHub issue directly — beads-sync handles issue closure when the beads ticket is closed and the PR is merged. Report that the issue was cleaned up and exit.
If the mapped beads ticket exists and is OPEN (work remaining): Proceed to ASSESS with this ticket.
If NO mapped beads ticket exists: Proceed to MIGRATE_ISSUE phase to create one.

phase.on-success: Ticket correlation established — proceed to ASSESS or MIGRATE_ISSUE.
phase.on-failure: Exit — no labeled issues found or command error.

phase.name: MIGRATE_ISSUE

actions.action: Determine the ticket type from the GitHub issue labels: - If labeled "bug" → type = bug - If labeled "enhancement" or "feature" → type = feature - Otherwise → type = task
Create a beads ticket from the GitHub issue content: bd create --type {type} --title "{issue title}" --description "{issue body}" Store the new ticket ID.
Add the label to the new beads ticket: bd label add {ticket-id} autonomously-nightly

Add a migration comment to the beads ticket (do NOT post directly to GitHub — beads-sync will propagate it as github-actions[bot]): bd comment add {ticket-id} "Migrated from GitHub issue #{number}. Work will proceed via the starterpack orchestration workflow." Remove the "autonomously-nightly" label from the GitHub issue since the ticket is now in beads: gh issue edit {number} --remove-label "autonomously-nightly"
Commit and push the beads metadata: git add .beads/ git commit -m "{ticket-id}: migrate GitHub issue #{number} to beads" git push

phase.on-success: Migration complete — proceed to ASSESS with the new ticket.

phase.name: ASSESS

actions.action: Apply the autonomous-nightly behavior's ticket-assessment rules to the ticket content retrieved in DETECT.
Determine whether the ticket has sufficient information for autonomous execution. Produce a verdict: COMPLETE or INCOMPLETE.

phase.on-success: If COMPLETE, proceed to ROUTE_COMPLETE. If INCOMPLETE, proceed to ROUTE_INCOMPLETE.
phase.on-failure: Exit and report the assessment failure.

phase.name: ROUTE_INCOMPLETE

actions.action: Apply the autonomous-nightly behavior's insufficient-information rules: formulate questions, add a comment to the beads ticket via "bd comment add", commit and push so beads-sync propagates the comment to GitHub.

phase.on-success: Exit — questions posted, no further action.
phase.on-failure: Report the failure to post questions and exit.

phase.name: ROUTE_COMPLETE

actions.action: Activate autonomous mode — all human gates in the standard workflow are auto-approved for this ticket. The following configuration is pre-set and not prompted: - Scope: single ticket (current ticket only). - Docs phase: run normally (scout, audit if needed, auto-approve changes).
Apply the autonomous-nightly behavior's pr-merge-rules for PR handling.
Log the autonomous mode activation in the status output.
actions.note: Activating autonomous mode also activates sub-agent dispatch mode for IMPLEMENTATION (see CLAUDE.md dispatch-mode). Agent Teams will not be used — all implementation sub-tasks use blocking Task calls.

phase.on-success: Proceed to the standard ENTRY lifecycle flow. The standard sequence (ENTRY branch setup, PLANNING, IMPLEMENTATION, DOCS, PR) runs with all human gates auto-approved.
phase.on-failure: Report the failure and exit. Do not proceed to the standard workflow.
